summary: Cute multi-step "Will you be my Valentine?" website with name collection, love-slider gate, heart animations, a final yes/no choice, a playful "wrong answer" branch, plus per-page animations and a final "Yes" confirmation page.
architectural_notes: All image assets should use transparent backgrounds (where applicable), rendered as static frontend assets.
imageRequirements:
## Edit Images
- Ensure fully transparent background/alpha channel (valentine-bg.dim_1920x1080.png > valentine-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
- Ensure fully transparent background/alpha channel (heart-mascot.dim_512x512.png > heart-mascot.dim_512x512.png)
- Ensure fully transparent background/alpha channel (mad-emoji.dim_256x256.png > mad-emoji.dim_256x256.png)

existing_features.title: Create a 3-step intro flow that runs before the main Valentine question page: (1) a page that asks for First Name, (2) a page that asks for Last Name, and (3) a page titled "How Much Do You Love Me?" that contains a heart-themed slider.

On the "How Much Do You Love Me?" slider page, prevent moving to the next page unless the slider is moved fully to the end (100%). When the slider reaches the end, play a hearts-filling-the-page animation, then automatically navigate to the main "Will you be my Valentine?" page.
Implement the main page with the question text "Will you be my Valentine?" and display the entered First Name and Last Name next to the question.

Add "Yes" and "No" options on the main Valentine page. If the user selects "No", navigate to a separate page that displays "Wrong answer!" plus a mad emoji and includes a "Go Back" button that returns to the main Valentine page.
Apply a cohesive cute visual theme across all pages (colors, typography, spacing, and components) with consistent heart/valentine styling.
Add generated static image assets to support the Valentine theme and render them in the UI from frontend/public/assets/generated (do not serve images via the backend).

Add noticeable, cute animations to each page in the Valentine flow (FirstNamePage, LastNamePage, LoveSliderPage, ValentineMainPage, WrongAnswerPage), including an on-enter transition for the main card/content so each step feels animated when it appears.
When the user presses "Yes" on the main Valentine page, navigate to a new final confirmation/celebration page instead of showing a browser alert.
Ensure all generated image assets used by the app have transparent backgrounds (alpha channel), including the background pattern image if it is retained as an image asset.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create 3-step intro flow: Page 1 asks First Name, Page 2 asks Last Name, then proceed to slider page
Add slider gate page titled "How Much Do You Love Me?" with heart-themed slider; only allow next when slider reaches the end
When slider reaches the end, play hearts-filling-page animation then navigate to main Valentine page
Main page: "Will you be my Valentine?" with cute styling; display entered First and Last name next to the question
Add Yes/No buttons; No routes to "Wrong answer!" page with mad emoji and a Go Back button
Add cute animations/transitions on each page (intro pages, slider gate, main question page, and wrong-answer page)
If user presses "Yes" on the main Valentine page, navigate to a final confirmation/celebration page

projectName: Cute multi-step Valentine website with name capture, love-slider gate, and yes/no flow
